claims.statement: AirDNA reports 5,949 active listings, $38.4K average trailing revenue, 50% available-night occupancy, $476 achieved ADR, and $215 RevPAR through June 2026; each was lower year over year, while Key Data's Q1 adjusted-paid occupancy uses a different managed-property method.

City rules limit vacation rentals to ancillary residential use, prohibit apartments, apply a 20% organized-neighborhood cap, and distinguish 26 annual contracts for new permittees from 32 plus four third-quarter contracts for existing permittees; no current legal count or property result was obtained.
Redfin reports a $658,606 all-home median over the three months ending May 2026, up 1.3% year over year; this broad summary is not a clean STR acquisition sample or purchase baseline.
PSP exposes 2026 monthly passenger and aircraft report links, but this run extracted no current passenger or feeder-market value and therefore makes no access-demand claim.

Freddie Mac's U.S. 30-year fixed average was 6.55% on July 16, 2026; AirDNA's national outlook expects slower 2026 demand, quicker supply, a slight occupancy dip, and a 2027 lift, none of which is a local forecast or investor quote.

The regional tourism directory shows luxury resorts, boutique hotels, villas, and vacation rentals across Greater Palm Springs, but this run obtained no Palm Springs city room count, occupancy, or ADR.

The regional calendar identifies fall-through-spring concentration and named events including Modernism Week, Coachella, Pride, and the film festival; these are timing context only, not causal uplift.

No target property or current quotes were supplied; management, property tax, STR insurance, maintenance, energy, and other property costs require explicit user inputs and remain missing rather than zero.
The attempted official extreme-heat page was not extractable, and no property-specific heat, insurance, downtime, or loss value is available.
Agent-authored remaining-2026 base assumes demand down 2%, supply down 2%, no ADR shock, and 167 available calendar nights; these are bounded scenario inputs, not observations.
Broad revenue, occupancy, ADR, and RevPAR declines make continued near-term softness plausible even though active supply also contracted.
Falsify the remaining-2026 base if two consecutive source-aligned monthly updates show occupancy and RevPAR above their prior-year levels without a definition change.
Agent-authored 2027 downside assumes demand down 8%, supply up 3%, ADR down 5%, and 365 available nights; it is not an observed forecast.
A downside remains plausible if local broad metrics keep weakening while national travel costs and supply pressure rise.
Falsify the downside if source-aligned local occupancy, ADR, and RevPAR recover above June 2026 levels for two consecutive quarters while active supply does not reaccelerate.
Agent-authored 2027 base assumes demand up 2%, flat supply, ADR up 1%, and 365 available nights; these values are sensitivity inputs.
A modest 2027 stabilization is plausible if the national occupancy recovery appears locally and Palm supply remains below its prior-year level.
Falsify the base if two consecutive quarters show source-aligned RevPAR still falling more than 10% year over year or active supply rising faster than demand.
Agent-authored 2027 upside assumes demand up 6%, supply down 2%, ADR up 4%, and 365 available nights; it is an explicit sensitivity case.
An upside requires broad local recovery, continued supply contraction, and event-season compression that is visible in aligned market metrics rather than assumed from event dates.
Falsify the upside if 2027 Q1 and Q2 source-aligned occupancy or RevPAR fail to exceed their 2026 counterparts or supply turns positive without stronger demand.

claims.statement: No public source fetched in this invocation supplies a dated, auditable Palm Springs 5+ entire-home luxury occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, or revenue series with qualifying sample n; those values remain unknown.
AirDNA reports 6% 5+ bedrooms and 91.4% entire homes as separate broad marginals; city certificate, cap, and contract rules do not provide a 5+ luxury legal count. The marginals are not multiplied.

The current filtered Redfin page reports 23 active 5+ house asks from $729,000 to $30,000,000, but plans, outliers, an outside-city result, unverified IDX data, and missing rental/legal/luxury gates make the sample unclean.
PSP publishes activity reports but no extracted segment-specific passenger or booking population supports luxury demand.
The 6.55% U.S. 30-year conventional average is financing context only; a large-luxury investor purchase requires a property- and borrower-specific quote.
Greater Palm Springs offers luxury resorts, villas, and boutique hotels across multiple cities, but no comparable Palm Springs 5+ villa performance sample was obtained.
Regional fall-through-spring events may define sensitivity windows, but no event is assigned a causal luxury occupancy or ADR effect.

Pool/spa, landscape, water, energy, management, certificate/compliance, property tax, association, insurance, and maintenance require explicit property-specific inputs; none is assigned a default or zero.
No property-specific heat, insurance, pool/landscape downtime, or loss value was obtained; the official heat page attempt was not extractable.
The only segment scenario is a user-entered sensitivity requiring base occupancy, ADR, purchase price, variable operating cost, and fixed-risk cost; it publishes no observed segment forecast.
Replace the unknown segment posture only when an accessible auditable 5+ entire-home qualifying sample supplies achieved performance, sample n, legal overlap, and matching definitions.

forecastMethods.label: User-entered unknown-segment sensitivity

limitations: No observed segment forecast or default economics.
